sentences.text: My name is Ester Krik.
When I was born, I was Ester [?
Wowk, ?] [?
W-O-W-K. ?]
My birthday is 1918, May the 10th.
I will be soon 77.
Now I'm 76.
I was born in Poland, Dubno.
We was six-children home, parents and four sisters and two brothers.
We was all in the same family, working all together.
We got like a little factory in the house.
A couple children was working in the house of sewing machines.
Someone went to the store, and a couple went to the markets.
Every day was a market of a different place.
Even if you want, I should name the places, I can name the places.
Monday was in Dubno.
Tuesday was in [INAUDIBLE].
Wednesday was in [INAUDIBLE].
And Thursday was in [INAUDIBLE].
And Saturday and Sunday we was home for the family, all together.
We wasn't rich, but a very close, nice family.
In my home we was spoken Jewish.
And we was a religious family.
We observed all the holidays and Saturdays.
Dubno was a small town near [INAUDIBLE] valley, near [INAUDIBLE] Lutsk.
Yes.
Yes.
There was a Jewish, nice population with organizations, with schools.
It was a very nice place, a nice Jewish community.
Yes.
I feel the anti-Semitism because we didn't want to go Saturday in school.
Saturday we want to stay home, and they insist we should go to school.
And who didn't went to school, they didn't listen to the teachers, so they got the lower mark.
But [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] we feel as very-- until the world wasn't so bad, but soon the Nazis came, so the Ukrainian, they start-- they wanted to take away everything.
And then the Nazis says, this will be to kill people a day.
So they kept longer, like a day.
They tried to give up a lot of Jewish families.
Yes.
Yes.
The Ukrainians.
The most was the Ukrainians.
And some people, they wanted to survive, so the Ukrainians [INAUDIBLE], they said they will help.
They took away everything that we gave them, they should help us.
But as soon as we gave them away everything, they gave us out.
First, they made a ghetto.
Later, they made a ghetto from [INAUDIBLE].
People where they have a [INAUDIBLE], like carpenters, painters, carpenters, sewing people.
So the people that they got the [INAUDIBLE], they put in a different side, in the live side.
And people that they didn't get the [INAUDIBLE], they was a business, they took another place straight to kill the people.
And we was in the ghetto till they said, [INAUDIBLE].
So my oldest brother, he was living 8 miles from Dubno.
He was married.
He had a wife and family, and he was living in a [?
Cesky ?] [? colony. ?] In this place was the name [?
Mirohoszcza. ?] In [?
Mirohoszcza ?], he lived there, and he helped to build an [INAUDIBLE], like a theater.
[PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS] So he know how this was built.
And then my brother was working in-- how they call a meal-- where they making flour and farina?
So my brother was working there, and the wife got a little store, and they make a very nice living.
But when they said, the Jews has to go in the ghettos, they took away everything, and they send the Jews in the ghettos.
When they made the ghettos free, my brother, was he was living in [?
Mirohoszcza. ?] He tried to help himself and Jewish children-- maybe we will go to [?
Mirohoszcza, ?] and we will work there-- to take out the potatoes and the beets-- to work, to help for them, everything of the land that has to be done.
So my brother took a lot of younger girls and boys, and we went to live in [?
Mirohoszcza. ?] We stand by the [INAUDIBLE], by the [?
Cesky ?] people.
They didn't pay us.
We didn't ask they should pay.
We was happy to be not in the ghetto.
And they gave us a little to eat, and we was working how much we could.
And we were sleeping in the garages.
And when the time came, when the Nazis says [? we'll ?] have to go back in the ghetto-- so they send them home.
They send us to the ghettos.
But we was hiding in the woods-- me, my brother, my sister-in-law, the family.
We was hiding in the woods.
It was very bad.
And we wanted to take out my mother and sister from the ghetto.
Also, my brother-- both brothers was with me.
So we send somebody, a good friend, he should take out my mother and my sister.
But they said-- by the ghetto, by the door-- they said, you can go in, but you cannot come out.
So my mother says she don't want to see her children get killed.
Stay there, that's where we will be.
I find out my mother and a sister, Fanny, they got killed.
And before they got killed, they took out-- my mother got a golden teeth, and my sister got a couple golden teeth.
Before they killed them, they took out the teeth, and they killed them.
And they were killed in the ghetto?
In the ghetto.
Me and both brothers, my sister-in-law, and the children-- my brother got five children-- and my sister-in-law's brother-- we was 10 people.
And we was hiding from one place to the other, one place to the other.
One time my brother says, we don't have any more other choice.
I know how they built the [INAUDIBLE] like a theater.
And I helped them build.
He was a member there because he was living there, and the children went to the school, and the sister-in-law got the little store.
So I know how the-- so we didn't get other choice.
And a Saturday, late in the afternoon, we went-- when it got a little bit dark, we went in.
And there's a little-- big windows, but you can knock out a little window, and open-- there was like a screw to open.
In this way, we opened one window, and we came in, all in the [INAUDIBLE].
And within this [INAUDIBLE] was a door to the cellar.
So we went into the cellar.
And with two knives and one [? top ?] we made a little hole in the cellar, and we made like a drawer to go in.
We made like a drawer.
With the drawer we went in, and we made another drawer at the top.
We put dark sand on it, as they shouldn't see us as a door.
In this way, we was laying there nine months.
Once a month, just once a month, when it was dark nights, my brother and brother-in-law went in to steal potatoes from the pigs where they was making-- also to bring water-- we should have something.
So we got one bag with 10 bottles, empty bottles.
So once a month, my brother and brother-in-law went.
And there was-- how to say which [INAUDIBLE] to say.
How to say-- there was a little-- where the water was running.
And there they filled up the bottles.
And with grass they closed the bottles.
And all the 10 bottles they put in a bag, and they put this in the woods.
Later they went to find a couple potatoes, to ask--
Yeah.
So they took-- how that goes-- like, where there was [? running ?], like, a little bridge was there.
And so then my brother and my sister-in-law's brother went to look in a dark night for potatoes, for bread, for water.
And one time-- and I was sitting always, I was sitting upstairs when they left.
It was dark.
I was sitting and wait for them-- when I will hear they coming over-- to open the window and to let them in.
Instead my brother and my sister-in-law's brother should came over, my brother by himself came over.
[INAUDIBLE] Yona-- his name was Yona-- [INAUDIBLE] Yona, he got killed on the way.
And my brother got just hit in this place.
He got shot.
We didn't get no medicine, nothing, and this healed.
And the brother got killed.
Later, we heard rumors, like the people was laughing, and they recognized him, and he is dead.
We couldn't help it.
When it was another dark night, both of my brothers went the same way to look for bread and this, and this.
Every day we was-- before 10 people, later nine people-- every day, we get one cup of water in the morning and a thin, thin, very thin slice of bread.
And my brother and my sister-in-law says, and we said to each other, give a sip, but remember, if you will give a big sip, we will not be for nine people, we will not have for tomorrow.
So everybody from this cup gave a little sip.
And this was once a day, it was.
This was our meal.
We didn't know when is night and when is day, but through the floor.
It was a wooden floor.
Through the floor, we saw when this is day, when the sun came in.
My brother's children heard all the [?
Cesky ?] children running around.
They went to school.
The coffee was smelling in the morning.
In the evenings, the suppers were smelling.
My brother's children always says, why?
Why we have to lay?
Why we have to suffer?
Why are we not allowed to be in the free because we're Jews?
So after everything, my sister-in-law became sick.
So sick that her head was like in a tub of water.
And in a short time, she died.
And this little bunker that we got, we couldn't stretch out the legs.
Everybody was sitting, laying this way.
In this little bunker, we made a hole, and we buried my sister-in-law.
After we buried my sister-in-law, my brother's oldest daughter, Miriam, got sick.
And she got sick-- to talk just about bread, coffee, milk, potato, this was-- and she wasn't allowed to talk because we're afraid somebody will hear the voices.
So we tried our best, and she choked, and she died.
We buried Miriam.
The latest was Sonia.
My brother's fourth child, the baby was Sonia.
And Sonia was five years old.
And she says, to the father, to me, to my brother, I will be very [? buried ?] like the mother, like Miriam-- because all of a sudden, she got paralyzed, and the girl couldn't stand up.
And my brother ask her, I will give you a piece of-- before they was asking for bread-- maybe I will give you a piece of bread, maybe I will give-- No, I don't want.
Sonia give me [INAUDIBLE].
She couldn't pick up then.
He tell Sonia, move this.
The girl died.
She asked if she will be buried like the mother, like the sister.
It was such a tragedy.
We went through so much.
After a while, we heard this was [INAUDIBLE] before the Nazis went back.
And so of our place, where we was laying in this [INAUDIBLE].
This was called an [INAUDIBLE] in [?
Mirohoszcza. ?] And we was laying there.
All of a sudden, we heard Nazis came over our head.
That's when they went back from the front, and they need a rest.
So they came upstairs.
We heard the Nazis.
We heard their walking.
We heard everything.
We couldn't, we wasn't allowed to-- but we was thinking, this is the end.
This is the end.
They were standing a day, and after the day, they left.
They left, and we was relieved a little bit.
Later, in a couple days, another company came.
And we didn't know what's going on because we was in the bunker.
We didn't saw nobody.
Nobody knew about us.
There was rumors that some here is hiding people, but they couldn't find, in the cellar, the hole where we was laying.
So after a while, they said, we heard rumors.
And another thing, my sister-in-law-- and I don't know if you will believe, if you will not believe-- my sister-in-law in the dreams called my brother.
And she told me, leave this place.
Leave this place because you have four people dead.
Leave this place.
So we was thinking, where we should go?
How she can say, leave this place?
She was here, and she knows the whole situation.
And she says, help yourself.
Leave this place.
So one time my oldest brother went [INAUDIBLE] to [? buzz ?] the people what he was-- did business with them.
And he went there.
The name is Vladich, and [?
Pruscia. ?] The name is [?
Pruscia ?] Vladich.
And he came one time, late in the night, throw a couple of potatoes, of bread.
So both brothers was there.
And they saw-- and this was a corner house.
And the corner house has so many lights, so light, so many people.
They was afraid.
They went in the-- where the horses was.
And they went there.
They was hiding.
And later they saw the son.
The son, Vladich, came out to say, Vladich, oh he says, why you came with so many people?
My brother says, how come I saw so light and-- he says his father died.
His father died.
My brother started to cry because he got his own pain.
And he got to crying, such a nice person, and this, and this.
He says to him, you know, Vladich, I have in the woods a place, and this is not finished yet.
Do me a favor, let me-- and they loved my sister-in-law and the children.
My sister-in-law wasn't alive anymore.
[DRINKS] [PAUSES FOR 5 SECONDS] Thank you.
And my sister-in-law was dead there, a couple months.
He says, I'm building a bunker.
And until the bunker will be finished, do me a favor.
Let me to come with my wife and the children.
He says, I am afraid.
Here ?] I got the mother with two daughters.
A mother with two sisters.
He says, I am afraid for my mother with the two sisters because in a short time-- there was a place, Malyn.
The name was Malyn.
And in Malyn was hiding a couple Jews.
So they burned the whole, the whole, the whole place.
They burned because they find out that the Malyn was hiding Jews.
So he  says, I am afraid, my mother and the sisters-- He says, we will be so quiet.
When you will have a chance to bring us something, it's good.
We will never go down.
Make for us a place on the-- how to call this of the-- the word?
In the ceiling, the loft?
In the ceiling.
Yeah.
There is a ladder for the animals, a ladder, things, straw, whatever thing.
So make there a place for us.
And I promise you, nobody will go down.
Nobody will know.
OK, luckily, he says, OK, come tomorrow night.
Tomorrow night we got just two knives and one pot and my brother's three children because he got five.
Sonia died.
Miriam died.
So it was [?
Manny ?] and Hanah [INAUDIBLE] and Monek.
So the two children, Monek and Hanah [INAUDIBLE] couldn't walk.
So both brothers hold them.
And I went with the oldest.
I went with Miriam.
Of course, it was one, two o'clock in the night, in a dark night.
And my both brothers was with beards.
I got the pig tails, but with knives my brothers took off my hair.
And we went to [INAUDIBLE] to Vladich.
And the place was the name [INAUDIBLE], a [?
Cesky ?] place.
And we-- on the way we saw two Nazis with a Russian.
So we was thinking, this is-- they will kill us.
But they could hear how we was frightened, how we was afraid.
And they was afraid for my brothers, or they were sad for us.
I don't know.
And we went this way.
We didn't say a word.
And they went this way.
They didn't touch us.
And we came up [INAUDIBLE], and the boy, Vladich, was waiting for us.
And we went up the stairs.
Tomorrow morning was a rumor that they saw Jews with two beards walking up the [INAUDIBLE].
And Vladich came over to my-- and they made up with my brother, they will not-- when he will come to feed the horse, he will not come.
He will make, uh-hmm, so we will know somebody's there.
So my brother when that-- he says, you know what?
Last night two Nazis saw people going, and two children on the--
He says, we didn't saw nobody, nobody saw us, and I don't know about nothing.
This same day, the people from when they went back, they made a factory in this [INAUDIBLE].
They made a factory for old cars, the cars that has to be fixed, and this, and this.
When this night, we wasn't able to go out, forget it.
It was the [INAUDIBLE], it was there day and night.
It was the people that they was watching the place.
And this night, if we wouldn't be able to go out, forget it.
We would have died there.
So we came and so my brother says, we don't now nobody.
The people, when they made in this [INAUDIBLE], when they made the factory for cars so a lot of the Nazis stayed there because they know how to work them.
So each boss, to each family, the [?
Cesky ?] families, they gave one or two Nazis to sleep and to eat.
Vladich came over, made uh-hmm.
And my brother went down and says, what's the matter?
You know what has happened?
In the [INAUDIBLE]-- and my brother makes believe he didn't know what is a [INAUDIBLE].
They made a factory for old cars, new cars.
And when the people going back from the front, they stay in there.
And day and night is the [INAUDIBLE].
And now I'm afraid because they gave to us two Nazis.
They should stay by us.
And they tell all the people, they have to sleep by us and to eat by us.
So we got, again, scared.
Every night we heard them going and coming, and they was whistling.
When they came they was whistling.
We heard them whistling.
Until one time there wasn't enough food for the [INAUDIBLE].
So Vladich says, what I should do?
I am afraid for my mother and sister.
And here is no more-- you cannot be upstairs.
My brother says, you know what?
Do me a favor.
I have-- give me a shovel, and I will make a little bunker downstairs.
He didn't get other choice.
He says, now I am even afraid to let you out because when they will find you, we're in big trouble all.
So it was a little relief for us.
He gave him a little shovel.
He made a little hole, and we went then downstairs.
The same--we made like a drawer.
After this, in a couple months, came the Russian.
We was with the Russian three days, and we was afraid to look out and what was.
But on the third day, Vladich came.
